steps.title: Identify the preference contrast
steps.content: The key signal is the contrast marked by “but”: “glossy ibises prefer areas with deep water during breeding season, but that was not true for small dark herons.” The word “but” highlights opposing preferences.

steps.title: Connect the contrast to the answer
steps.content:
Strategies that increase water depth would be more likely to attract glossy ibises than small dark herons, since ibises prefer deep water and herons do not.

Choices B, C, and D either ignore the research (introducing non-wetland birds or lifespan effects) or contradict it (falsely claiming decreased appeal).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
